pr.title: chore(admin): static-analysis audit of /admin/settings fields (P8)
pr.body:
## Summary

Read-only sweep of every column the `app_settings` singleton row exposes through `PATCH /api/admin/settings`. For each field, the audit records the form's stated intent, every backend read site, whether changing the value in the form has any real effect, and a verdict (`WORKING` / `WORKING (conditional)` / `INERT (display only)` / `DEAD CODE`).

Output is a single markdown doc: `docs/audit/admin-settings-audit.md`.

**This PR adds documentation only.** No code or schema changes — all proposed fixes are deferred to the three follow-up PRs listed at the end of the doc.

### Findings the operator can act on

1. **`invoice_commission_percent` — DEAD CODE.** Zero consumers in `backend/app/services_*.py`, `backend/app/routers/*.py`, `cryptopay.py`, or anywhere else. The column is on the model, the form persists edits, the audit log records the diff — but no business code reads the value. Setting a non-zero number here is a silent UX-trap: the admin thinks invoices will carry a platform fee; they won't.

2. **`min_deposit` (singleton) — INERT.** The wallet deposit path (`services_wallet.create_deposit_invoice`, line 296) enforces **`currency.min_deposit`**, the per-currency override. The singleton value is read only by `routers/admin/settings.py:75` to render the form. The model docstring at `models.py:669-676` acknowledges this is "a global default kept around for admin display"; the admin form doesn't. Changing it does nothing.

3. **`min_withdraw` (singleton) — INERT.** Same pattern — `services_wallet.create_withdrawal` (line 916) enforces `currency.min_withdraw`, not `AppSettings.min_withdraw`.

### Fields confirmed wired correctly

`deal_commission_percent` (deals), `vip_commission_percent` (deals, gated on VIP), `inactivity_pending_confirmation_days` + `inactivity_pending_cancellation_days` (sweep loop), `max_active_services_per_user` (services router), `auto_withdraw_enabled` (admin withdrawals + services_wallet), `maintenance_enabled` + `maintenance_message` (maintenance middleware + bot mirror).

Every field has its read sites cited line-by-line in the doc.

### Recommended follow-up PRs (named in the doc)

- `chore/drop-invoice-commission-percent` — drop the dead column end-to-end (low risk, mechanical).
- `fix/admin-settings-min-amounts-inert` — either wire the singleton `min_deposit`/`min_withdraw` in as a global floor (Option A), or relabel them as informational and link to `/admin/taxonomy?tab=currencies` (Option B, cheaper). Calling this a product decision in the doc rather than committing to one path here.
- `chore/admin-settings-help-text` — add tooltips for the non-obvious fields (the `−1` sentinel on `vip_commission_percent`, the silent-fallback semantics of `auto_withdraw_enabled` when `CRYPTOBOT_TOKEN` is unset).

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Spot-check the citations in the doc against the linked files. The big claim is "`invoice_commission_percent` has zero consumers"; the quickest verification is `rg invoice_commission_percent backend/app | grep -v 'models.py\|seed.py\|schemas.py\|routers/admin/settings.py'` — should return no results.
- [ ] Read the **Recommendations** section and tell me which follow-up PR(s) you want me to open next. The `min_deposit` / `min_withdraw` fix has a product decision baked in (Option A "wire it in" vs Option B "relabel as informational") that I'd rather have you weigh in on before I write code.
- [ ] Sanity-check the per-field verdicts in the TL;DR table — if any of them feel off based on production behaviour you've observed, ping me and I'll dig in.

### Notes

No behaviour changes in this PR, so CI failures (if any) would only be cosmetic (markdown lint, link checking). Pre-commit hooks run only against the markdown file.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/18a86fe3f3364c29a771ba31944c295d
Requested by: @g2void2-byte
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
